result.meeting_context: Development Review Committee meeting for two projects: 500 Ellis St. planned hotel and office building in East Wisman neighborhood, and 1040 Terra Bella Ave. personal storage buildings returning for final review.

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Project site currently has 2 single story office buildings with surface parking that will be removed, with trees preserved around perimeter and one center tree
points.source: Tom Jess : So the project site currently is 2 single story office buildings with surface parking. Here's a couple images of what's there and here's a site plan. However, we are doing our best to preserve as many of the trees as possible.

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Proposed development includes six story hotel on corner of Ellis and National, two-story office building, with hotel lobby entrance on corner and guest pickup/dropoff in back
points.source: Tom Jess : The proposed site plan is for a six story hotel here on the corner of Ellison National and then a two-story office building a little further down Ellis with a the hotel lobby entrance is on the corner.

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Parking utilizes mechanical automated system located centrally, allowing active uses around building perimeter rather than parking
points.source: Tom Jess : So the parking is shielded and sheltered so it's hidden in the back. But the parking is central to the building and that allowed us to line the building around the sides with with active uses

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Concern about bike room location being too far from Ellis Street frontage, potentially forcing bicycles wrong way down drive or onto narrow sidewalk
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : One of the things that actually highlighted for me though is a little bit of concern about how far back from the Ellis St. frontage the secure bike room is.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Paseo entry point at Ellis needs rethinking due to heavy focus on circulation rather than welcoming feel, with grading creating barriers
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : And at the frontage I feel like feel like there's it. I'm a little bit concerned that the sort of extent of planter and sort of stair and pathway it is, is such that we're we're losing some of that welcoming feel

points.speaker: Linda Pomancini
points.text: Recommendation to push stairs and ramps further back to create significant landing area that feels part of Ellis sidewalk rather than private space
points.source: Linda Pomancini : So maybe there's a way to push those elements back further so that as you come in, you have a significant, what I'll call landing area.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: City Council defined objective for projects to aim for 75% California native plants across entirety of planting programs
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : Additionally and this is something that's come up more recently on projects, our City Council has defined an objective for projects to to aim for 75% California native plants

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Strong objectives to maximize tree canopy in redevelopment projects, starting with preserving healthy existing canopy and incorporating new larger canopy trees
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : We have very very strong objectives to try to maximize tree canopy and and redevelopment projects. You know a great first step is preserving the healthy tree canopy that you already have.

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Hotel design emphasizes prominent key corner at National and Ellis with unique materials, different lobby entrance, and tallest massing element
points.source: Tom Jess : So when we look at massing, we kind of this is for the hotel building obviously the the prominent key corner here, it plays a big role, it's the tallest part.

points.speaker: Russell Sherman
points.text: Diagram shows unified street wall but elevation reads as three distinct vertical pieces rather than single mass with prominent corner
points.source: Russell Sherman : But when I read the elevation in perspective, right, what I really get is 3 vertical pieces on the facade, right? That is not reading as a piece.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Need to study ways to simplify street wall design while maintaining building base breaks, allowing key corner to stand out rather than striped progression
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : And So what we want you to study is sort of ways to you know still give a well defined particularly building base that has those breaks and recesses that we desire

points.speaker: Russell Sherman
points.text: Office building's framed element appears most prominent rather than corner entry, contradicting stated design intent for prominent corner
points.source: Russell Sherman : In the office building, the architecture in my mind this is the most prominent piece because it is coming forward. It is the framed element, it is the thing.

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Design intent was to give office building visual weight through strong statement so it wouldn't get lost relative to hotel scale
points.source: Tom Jess : In order to give it some visual weight, we've had to, we tried to make a big strong statement on that one so that it wouldn't get lost relative to the scale, just the scale of the hotel.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Building base feels disconnected from northeast corner due to color/material treatment connecting to recessed volume above rather than corner
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So like when when when I look at that elevation, I don't see the diagram at all because the you know color and material of the the ground plane in the center is you know connected to the the the recessed plane of the volume

points.speaker: Russell Sherman
points.text: Suggestion to increase glazing proportions on hotel's upper white volume to reduce visual weight, similar to precise plan precedents
points.source: Russell Sherman : I'm wondering if just a step more glazing on that upper portion separates it a little bit, just separates a little bit.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Encourage creativity in hotel window design to make it feel less recognizably hotel-like in typical negative manner
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : But I'd really like to encourage you to to think about how there there can be some creativity in the window design to to make it feel a little bit less recognizably hotel like

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Metallic trespa treatment at corner may need something more different, with next level of detailing around windows for material introduction
points.source: Russell Sherman : I I think the next, the the next, you know there's there's a level of detailing for instance around windows and and these things which can allow for the introduction of other materials

points.speaker: Bryan Miranda
points.text: Project collaboration with Alta Housing involves donating land to increase affordable housing bedroom count in exchange for site densification
points.source: Bryan Miranda : This project is a collaboration with Alta Housing where we're donating land to Ulta Housing to increase their affordable housing bedroom count.

points.speaker: Bryan Miranda
points.text: Two-phase project with six story building (phase one) and four story building (phase two), totaling just over 400,000 square feet
points.source: Bryan Miranda : The building on the left is phase one, building on the right is phase two. Total building area just over 400,000 square feet.

points.speaker: Bryan Miranda
points.text: Planning Commission and City Council provided unanimous support for architectural design, expressing surprise that storage building could look this good
points.source: Bryan Miranda : But both through Planning Commission and City Council, we had unanimous support for the architectural design. Almost to the person, they were shocked that a storage building could look this good.

points.speaker: Jonathan Thomas
points.text: Simplified CMU split face base on all elevations and made vertical elements more symmetrical to create regular rhythm and pattern
points.source: Jonathan Thomas : So we've simplified that on both buildings, all elevations to a more solid base because we still have these vertical elements that come down and touch the ground that help give the rhythm and pattern.

points.speaker: Jonathan Thomas
points.text: Extended mural on western elevation and made it larger with mirrored balance on either side for better proportions
points.source: Jonathan Thomas : So we made that change, extended that down and have that mirrored and made that mural larger.

points.speaker: Jonathan Thomas
points.text: Shifted glazing on Building 2 to align with vertical elements above and continued purple accent around corners for elevation continuity
points.source: Jonathan Thomas : And we shifted some of the glazing as well. So we had some more storefront that was spandrel along San Rafael and it wasn't aligned with the vertical elements above.

points.speaker: Jonathan Thomas
points.text: Reduced parking stalls on both buildings to add more trees, screen trash enclosure, and increase canopy coverage along property lines
points.source: Jonathan Thomas : So overall we reduced 4 stalls on building 1 here and added a lot of trees screen the crash enclosure and we think overall this works pretty well

points.speaker: Jonathan Thomas
points.text: Landscape architect reviewed planting list to maximize native species while ensuring trees will thrive and provide adequate canopy over time
points.source: Jonathan Thomas : So the landscape architect reviewed the planting list and spent a lot of time making sure we had as many native species as possible.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Recommendation to work with landscape team to intersperse taller trees along highway frontage where appropriate, beyond smaller ironwood trees
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : I I would encourage as a a final recommendation for you for you to to work with your landscape team to see if like even in just like a few few appropriate locations along that stretch you might be able to find and and intersperse a tree that's a bit taller

points.speaker: Linda Pomancini
points.text: Building 2 south elevation inverted L features should be pushed further apart to create wider middle section and better facade rhythm
points.source: Linda Pomancini : The comment on the South elevation is that that we were thinking those inverted LS if they were pushed further apart so that that that middle piece that has the the seven on it were was a little wider

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Purple accent color appears too light and pastel, recommendation to evaluate darker neutral grey alternative through mock-up inspection
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So yeah, I think we'll we'll we'll make the recommendation to evaluate the the purple through a mock up inspection with an alternative, you know neutral, neutral, grey accent as an alternative.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Encourage pickup of elevational and landscape changes before public hearings so decision makers can see final anticipated product
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So if you can pick up some of these particularly the elevational changes and landscape changes ahead of ahead of the public hearings, I think it'll be beneficial

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: Recommendation for 500 Ellis team to work at schematic level with project planner to avoid trading extensive paper revisions
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So you know we are happy and encourage you to sort of you know work at a, you know a more schematic sketchy level with studying you know revisions and and collaborating with your project planner

points.speaker: Tom Jess
points.text: Appreciation expressed for working with Ellen, describing her as superstar who brought great help to project
points.source: Tom Jess : And and it'd be. I'd be a miss if I didn't mention how great it has been working with Ellen. She's a superstar and we really appreciate all the help that she's brought to the table so far.

points.speaker: Rebecca Shapiro
points.text: DRC meeting adjourned after completing review of both agenda items
points.source: Rebecca Shapiro : I think we'll be adjourning the N2 DRC Meeting.

decisions.text: Team will reconsider bike room placement to prioritize safe and convenient bike access to secure parking areas
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So I would do some reconsideration of of where you're placing that bike room to to to try to prioritize sort of safe and convenient bike access to the secure parking areas

decisions.text: Team will rethink Paseo entry point at Ellis to make it more open and welcoming, including grading and access design
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : Yeah I mean I think that that could be part of the but yeah the the objective is to to be really really much you know rethink that that entry point

decisions.text: Team will incorporate 75% California native plants target into planting programs and work towards maximizing tree canopy
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So we'll we'll want to see you incorporating that information into your plans and working working towards that that 75% target as well.

decisions.text: Team will study hotel street wall simplification options while maintaining building base breaks to let key corner stand out
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : And So what we want you to study is sort of ways to you know still give a well defined particularly building base that has those breaks and recesses that we desire

decisions.text: Team will make office building lobby entrance more prominent while maintaining strong visual statement for building presence
decisions.source: Tom Jess : But I mean we could make that statement and still make the the lobby entrance more prominent. We can certainly do that.

decisions.text: Team will work with project planner Ellen at schematic level on revisions before finalizing detailed drawings
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So we encourage you to work with your your project planner in that manner.

decisions.text: Team will push inverted L features further apart on Building 2 south elevation to create better facade rhythm and proportions
decisions.source: Linda Pomancini : The comment on the South elevation is that that we were thinking those inverted LS if they were pushed further apart

decisions.text: Team will evaluate purple accent color through mock-up inspection with darker neutral grey alternative
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : So yeah, I think we'll we'll we'll make the recommendation to evaluate the the purple through a mock up inspection with an alternative, you know neutral, neutral, grey accent as an alternative.

decisions.text: Team will work with staff on interim frontage planting along San Rafael if phase two landscaping cannot be incorporated into phase one timeline
decisions.source: Rebecca Shapiro : But barring that we are recommending that you work with with staff on on interim planting along that frontage
